shots.title: Handicap Trend API — per-sprint time-series of handicap, fairway%, GIR%
shots.notes: O(n) incremental computeHandicapTrend(). Running sums for cumulative averages. Integrated into ReportData and SlopeStats. 4 tests.

shots.title: Sprint Velocity Tracking — tickets/sprint trend with improving/stable/declining detection
shots.notes: computeVelocity() with 0.3 threshold for trend noise avoidance. VelocityReport type with points, avg_tickets, par_accuracy_pct, avg_differential, trend. 6 tests.

shots.title: Guard Effectiveness Metrics — JSONL recording + pure analytics function

hazards.description: [code review] GuardDecision type gap — no shared union between recordGuardExecution JSONL values and computeGuardMetrics switch. Also allow+context conflation undercounts context injections.
[code review] GuardMetricLine.tool typed as required string but undefined for Stop/PreCompact events — type lie in interface

shots.notes: recordGuardExecution() fire-and-forget appendFileSync to .slope/guard-metrics.jsonl. computeGuardMetrics() pure function with malformed-line resilience. GuardDecision union type added post-review. 9 tests.

shots.title: Dashboard Widgets — SVG charts for trend, velocity, guard effectiveness

hazards.description: [code review] New render functions lack metaphor parameter — inconsistent with existing renderHandicapTrendChart, renderSprintTable etc. which all accept optional metaphor

shots.notes: renderTrendTimeSeriesChart (multi-line SVG), renderVelocityChart (bars + differential overlay + summary cards), renderGuardEffectivenessChart (horizontal stacked bars). Guard section conditional. /api/guard-metrics route. 6 tests.

nutrition.description: Test coverage strong — 28 new tests, all passing. Full suite 2720 tests green.
Clean build, typecheck, zero regressions. Pure analytics module with no I/O.
Review findings addressed same-session — GuardDecision type, tool coercion, 3 test gap fixes

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Smooth sprint — analytics.ts is a clean pure-compute module. The pattern of types+compute in analytics, render in report, wire in dashboard is repeatable.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: New render functions should accept optional metaphor parameter for label consistency. The report.ts module is growing (776 lines) — consider extracting charts to charts.ts before next analytics sprint.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: Guard metrics JSONL approach was simpler than expected — appendFileSync in try/catch is robust for hot-path telemetry. The fire-and-forget pattern from transcript.ts transfers perfectly.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: S66 unblocks S70 (Session Insights & Debugging). The analytics pattern is extensible — adding new metrics follows the same 5-step recipe.

yardage_book_updates: New render functions must accept optional metaphor param — all existing chart renderers do
report.ts approaching god-module territory at 776 lines — extract chart renderers before next analytics sprint
GuardResult.decision is 'allow'|'deny'|'ask' but JSONL records synthetic 'context' and 'silent' values — use GuardDecision union
course_management_notes: T1→T3→T2→T4 order worked well — analytics functions built first, then dashboard wiring
Guard JSONL separate from ReportData by design — different data domains (runtime telemetry vs scorecard analytics)
Defensive sort in analytics functions is correct tradeoff — O(n log n) on small n preserves function purity
